Name: MizanQA
Description: MizanQA is the first benchmark designed to evaluate large language models (LLMs) on Moroccan legal question answering tasks.
Paper Title: MizanQA: Benchmarking Large Language Models on Moroccan Legal Question Answering
Venue Name: Conference of the European Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics
Abstract: The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has significantly propelled progress in natural language processing (NLP). However, their effectiveness in specialized, low-resourced domains—such as Arabic legal contexts—remains limited. This paper introduces MizanQA, a benchmark designed to evaluate LLMs on Moroccan legal question answering (QA) tasks, characterised by rich linguistic and legal complexity. The dataset draws on Modern Standard Arabic, Islamic Maliki jurisprudence, Moroccan customary law, and French legal influences. Comprising over 1,700 multiple-choice questions, including multi-answer formats, MizanQA captures the nuances of authentic legal reasoning. Benchmarking experiments with multilingual and Arabic-focused LLMs reveal substantial performance gaps, highlighting the need for tailored evaluation metrics and culturally grounded, domain-specific LLM development.
